```{r ancova, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}
label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 5"] <- label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 5"] + 2

# HTML formula – ANCOVA version
formula_label <- "<span style='font-size:18pt'><i>y<sub>ij</sub></i> = <i>β<sub>0j</sub></i> + <i>β<sub>1</sub></i><i>x<sub>ij</sub></i> + <i>e<sub>ij</sub></i></span>"

p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y, color = group, group = group)) +
  
  # pooled OLS through all raw points
  #geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = FALSE,
  #            color = "#D9D9D9", linewidth = 1.8) +
  
  # regression line through group means
  #geom_smooth(data = group_means,
  #            aes(x = x, y = y, group = 1),
  #            method = "lm", se = FALSE,
  #            color = "black", linewidth = 2, linetype = "solid") +
  
  # raw points, faded
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.20) +
  
  # ── PARALLEL within-group lines (ANCOVA) ───
  geom_line(data = line_df,
            aes(x = x, y = y, color = group, group = group),
            linewidth = 1.8, linetype = "solid") +
  
  # group mean points
  #geom_point(data = group_means,
  #           aes(x = x, y = y, color = group),
  #           size = 8, alpha = 1, shape = 19) +
  
  geom_text(
    data     = label_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = group),
    hjust    = -.10,
    fontface = "bold",
    size     = 6,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  
  annotate(
    "richtext",
    x         = min(df$x),
    y         = max(df$y),
    label     = formula_label,
    hjust     = 0.00,
    vjust     = 1,
    fill      = NA,
    label.color = NA
  ) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(floor(min(df$x)), ceiling(max(df$x) + 5), by = 10),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.20))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(floor(min(df$y)), ceiling(max(df$y) + 5), by = 5),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.20))
  ) +
  
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.title      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text       = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks      = element_line(color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  
  labs(x = "Predictor (X)", y = "Outcome (Y)")

#p

ggsave(here::here('slides','img','ancova_plot.png'),
       plot = p, width = 6.5, height = 4, dpi = 300)

```

.center[
<img src=`r here::here('slides','img','ancova_plot.png')` style="width:600px; display:block; margin:-40px auto -20px auto;">
]  

<small>

**Same model - different interpretation and motivation.**

<small>

- Cannot model varying slopes across groups  
- Cannot include level-2 predictors of group effects  
- Group differences are controlled, not modeled

</small></small>

---

## `r emo::ji("jigsaw")` Post-adjustment?

---

## `r emo::ji("jigsaw")` Post-adjustment?

**If nesting biases standard errors, adjust them directly.**

<small><small>

**R code:**

<pre style="font-size:0.8em;">
pooled_lm <- lm(y ~ x_c, data = df)
cluster_se <- sandwich::vcovCL(pooled_lm, cluster = df$group, type = "HC1")
</pre>

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

library(dplyr)
library(lmtest)
library(sandwich)
library(knitr)

set.seed(1234)

n_groups    <- 5
n_per_group <- 80
group_x_means    <- c(15, 30, 45, 60, 75)
group_intercepts <- c(8, 5, 2, -1, -4)
group_slopes     <- c(0.10, 0.10, 0.10, 0.10, 0.10)

df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n_groups, function(g) {
  x_g <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = group_x_means[g], sd = 20)
  y_g <- group_intercepts[g] + group_slopes[g] * x_g + rnorm(n_per_group, 0, 2)
  data.frame(group = factor(g), x = x_g, y = y_g)
}))

# ---------------------------
# 1. Center predictor
# ---------------------------
df <- df %>%
  mutate(x_c = x - mean(x))

# 1. Fit pooled OLS
pooled_lm <- lm(y ~ x_c, data = df)

# 2. Cluster-robust SEs (Huber-White / sandwich)
cluster_se <- vcovCL(pooled_lm, cluster = df$group, type = "HC1")

# 3. Extract coefficient table with robust SEs
robust_mat <- coeftest(pooled_lm, vcov. = cluster_se)

# 4. Convert to data frame
robust_table <- data.frame(
  term      = rownames(robust_mat),
  estimate  = robust_mat[, "Estimate"],
  std.error = robust_mat[, "Std. Error"],
  t_value   = robust_mat[, "t value"],
  p_value   = robust_mat[, "Pr(>|t|)"],
  row.names = NULL
)

# 5. Round for display
robust_table <- robust_table %>%
  mutate(
    estimate  = round(estimate, 3),
    std.error = round(std.error, 3),
    t_value   = round(t_value, 2),
    p_value   = round(p_value, 3)
  )

# 6. Show nicely in table
kable(robust_table, caption = "Fixed-effects Regression with Cluster-Robust SEs")

```

- The new SE = **0.02** up from **0.006**

- Does **not model cluster-specific effects** 

- Generally **not recommended** for multilevel data

</small></small>

---

## `r emo::ji("twisted_rightwards_arrows")` Separate regressions? 

---

## `r emo::ji("twisted_rightwards_arrows")` Separate regressions?

```{r seperate, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext)
set.seed(1234)

n_groups    <- 5
n_per_group <- 80

group_x_means    <- c(15, 30, 45, 60, 75)
group_intercepts <- c(8, 5, 2, -1, -4)
group_slopes     <- c(0.10, 0.10, 0.10, 0.10, 0.10)

df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n_groups, function(g) {
  x_g <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = group_x_means[g], sd = 20)  # wide sd = lots of overlap
  y_g <- group_intercepts[g] + group_slopes[g] * x_g + rnorm(n_per_group, 0, 2)
  data.frame(group = factor(g), x = x_g, y = y_g)
}))

group_colors <- c("#E41A1C","#377EB8","#4DAF4A","#FF7F00","#984EA3")

# group means
group_means <- aggregate(cbind(x, y) ~ group, data = df, FUN = mean)

label_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n_groups, function(g) {
  sub <- df[df$group == g, ]
  data.frame(
    group = factor(g),
    x     = max(sub$x) + 2.5,
    y     = max(sub$y)-3,
    label = paste0("Group ", g),
    color = group_colors[g]
  )
}))

label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 4"] <- 
  label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 4"] + 1

label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 2"] <- 
  label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 2"] + 0.50

label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 5"] <- 
  label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 5"] + 2

# HTML formula using ggtext richtext
formula_label <- "<span style='font-size:18pt'><i>y<sub>ij</sub></i> = <i>β<sub>0</sub></i> + <i>β<sub>1</sub></i><i>x<sub>ij</sub></i> + <i>e<sub>ij</sub></i></span>"


p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y, color = group, group = group)) +
  
  # regression line through group means (aggregated)
  geom_smooth(data = group_means,
              aes(x = x, y = y, group = 1),
              method = "lm", se = FALSE,
              color = "black", linewidth = 2, linetype = "solid") +
  
  # raw points, faded
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.10) +
  
  # within-group regression lines
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE,
              linewidth = 1.8, linetype = "solid") +
  
  # pooled OLS through all raw points
  #geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = FALSE,
  #            color = "#222222", linewidth = 1.8) +
  
  # group mean points
  geom_point(data = group_means,
             aes(x = x, y = y, color = group),
             size = 8, alpha = 1, shape = 19) +
  
  geom_text(
    data     = label_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = group),
    hjust    = 0,
    fontface = "bold",
    size     = 6,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # richtext annotation for the formula
  annotate(
    "richtext",
    x         = min(df$x),
    y         = max(df$y),
    label     = formula_label,
    hjust     = -0.25,
    vjust     = 1,
    fill      = NA,       # no box background
    label.color = NA      # no box border
  ) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      floor(min(df$x)),
      ceiling(max(df$x)+5),
      by = 10                                 # more ticks on y
    ),   # more ticks on x
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.20))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      floor(min(df$y)),
      ceiling(max(df$y)+5),
      by = 5                                 # more ticks on y
    ),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.20))
  )+
  
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.title      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text       = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks      = element_line(color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  
  labs(x = "Predictor (X)", y = "Outcome (Y)")

#p

ggsave(here::here('slides','img','seperate_plot.png'),
       plot = p, width = 6.5, height = 4, dpi = 300)

```

.center[
<img src=`r here::here('slides','img','seperate_plot.png')` style="width:600px; display:block; margin:-40px auto -20px auto;">
] 

**Analyze each level-2 unit separately.**

- 5 groups = 5 models

---

.center[
## `r emo::ji("twisted_rightwards_arrows")` Seperate regressions?

<small>

**5 separate regressions `r ji('arrow_right')` get the variance across groups** 

]

<small>

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

library(dplyr)
library(broom)
library(knitr)

# ---------------------------
# 1. Fit separate regressions per group
# ---------------------------
group_coefs <- df %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  do({
    fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = .)
    tidy(fit) %>%
      dplyr::select(term, estimate) %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate)
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

# Rename for clarity
colnames(group_coefs) <- c("group", "intercept", "slope")

# ---------------------------
# 2. Compute group-level summary statistics
# ---------------------------
coef_summary <- group_coefs %>%
  summarise(
    intercept_var = var(intercept),
    slope_var     = var(slope),
    intercept_slope_cov = cov(intercept, slope)
  )

```

.pull-left-45[

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

# ---------------------------
# 3. Show results
# ---------------------------
group_coefs2 <- group_coefs %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    intercept = round(intercept, 2),
    slope   = round(slope, 2)
  )

kable(group_coefs2, caption = "Separate Regressions per Group: Intercepts and Slopes") 

```

]

.pull-right-45[

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

# ---------------------------
# 3. Show results
# ---------------------------
coef_summary2 <- coef_summary %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    I_Var = round(intercept_var, 3),
    S_Var = round(slope_var, 5),
    IS_Cov = round(intercept_slope_cov, 3)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(I_Var, S_Var, IS_Cov)

kable(coef_summary2, caption = "Variance and Covariance Across Groups (equal weight)") 

```

]

</small></small>

---

## `r emo::ji("twisted_rightwards_arrows")` Separate regressions?

- Examine variance of intercepts & slopes

- All clusters are weighted equally  

- Does **not partition variance** 

- Involves estimating a **large number of parameters!** 

> 50 hospitals = 50 separate regressions = 500 parameters - this requires a ton of data! `r ji('pensive')` 

---

## `r ji('raised_eyebrow')` Which Effect Is Correct?

```{r paradox, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext)
set.seed(1234)
n_groups    <- 5
n_per_group <- 80
group_x_means    <- c(15, 30, 45, 60, 75)
group_intercepts <- c(8, 5, 2, -1, -4)
group_slopes     <- c(0.10, 0.10, 0.10, 0.10, 0.10)
df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n_groups, function(g) {
  x_g <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = group_x_means[g], sd = 20)
  y_g <- group_intercepts[g] + group_slopes[g] * x_g + rnorm(n_per_group, 0, 2)
  data.frame(group = factor(g), x = x_g, y = y_g)
}))
group_colors <- c("#E41A1C","#377EB8","#4DAF4A","#FF7F00","#984EA3")

# group means
group_means <- aggregate(cbind(x, y) ~ group, data = df, FUN = mean)

# ── ANCOVA: fit model with common slope ──────────────────────────────────────
ancova_fit <- lm(y ~ x + group, data = df)   # parallel slopes model
common_slope <- coef(ancova_fit)["x"]

# Build predicted line segments for each group over its x-range
line_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n_groups, function(g) {
  sub <- df[df$group == g, ]
  x_seq <- seq(min(sub$x), max(sub$x), length.out = 100)
  # predict with group factor set to g
  nd <- data.frame(x = x_seq, group = factor(g, levels = 1:n_groups))
  data.frame(group = factor(g), x = x_seq, y = predict(ancova_fit, newdata = nd))
}))

label_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n_groups, function(g) {
  sub <- df[df$group == g, ]
  data.frame(
    group = factor(g),
    x     = max(sub$x) + 2.5,
    y     = max(sub$y) - 3,
    label = paste0("Group ", g),
    color = group_colors[g]
  )
}))
label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 4"] <- label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 4"] + 1
label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 2"] <- label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 2"] + 0.50
label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 5"] <- label_df$y[label_df$label == "Group 5"] + 2

# HTML formula – ANCOVA version
formula_label <- "<span style='font-size:18pt'><i>y<sub>ij</sub></i> = <i>β<sub>0</sub></i> + <i>β<sub>1</sub></i><i>x<sub>ij</sub></i> + <i>α<sub>j</sub></i> + <i>e<sub>ij</sub></i></span>"

p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y, color = group, group = group)) +
  
  # pooled OLS through all raw points
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = FALSE,
              color = "#D9D9D9", linewidth = 1.8) +
  
  # regression line through group means
  geom_smooth(data = group_means,
              aes(x = x, y = y, group = 1),
              method = "lm", se = FALSE,
              color = "black", linewidth = 2, linetype = "solid") +
  
  # raw points, faded
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.10) +
  
  geom_line(data = line_df,
            aes(x = x, y = y, color = group, group = group),
            linewidth = 1.8, linetype = "solid") +
  
  # group mean points
  geom_point(data = group_means,
             aes(x = x, y = y, color = group),
             size = 8, alpha = 1, shape = 19) +
  
  geom_text(
    data     = label_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = group),
    hjust    = 0,
    fontface = "bold",
    size     = 6,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  
  #annotate(
  #  "richtext",
  #  x         = min(df$x),
  #  y         = max(df$y),
  #  label     = formula_label,
  #  hjust     = -0.35,
  #  vjust     = 1,
  #  fill      = NA,
  #  label.color = NA
  #) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(floor(min(df$x)), ceiling(max(df$x) + 5), by = 10),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.20))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(floor(min(df$y)), ceiling(max(df$y) + 5), by = 5),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.20))
  ) +
  
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.title      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text       = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks      = element_line(color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  
  labs(x = "Predictor (X)", y = "Outcome (Y)")

# p

ggsave(here::here('slides','img','paradox_plot.png'), plot = p, width = 6.5, height = 4, dpi = 300)

```

.center[

<img src=`r here::here('slides','img','paradox_plot.png')` style="width:600px; display:block; margin:-40px auto -20px auto;">

]  

<small>

**What happens with the same data can be confusing.** 

> Ignoring clustering changes the estimated relationship.

<style="clear: both;">

.center[
.highlight[
**<span style="color:#FFFFFF;">
We need a model that separates within- and between-group effects.
</span>**
]
]

</small>

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">  The Solution: Multilevel Models

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">  The Solution: Multilevel Models

```{r mlm, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext)

set.seed(1234)
n_groups    <- 5
n_per_group <- 80

group_x_means    <- c(15, 30, 45, 60, 75)
group_intercepts <- c(8, 5, 2, -1, -4)
group_slopes     <- c(0.05, 0.14, 0.08, 0.16, 0.03)
sigma_eps        <- 2   # residual SD — must match data generation

df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n_groups, function(g) {
  x_g <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = group_x_means[g], sd = 20)
  y_g <- group_intercepts[g] + group_slopes[g] * x_g + rnorm(n_per_group, 0, sigma_eps)
  data.frame(group = factor(g), x = x_g, y = y_g)
}))

# ── Population-level parameters ───────────────────────────────────────────────
beta0    <- mean(group_intercepts)   # mean intercept  (fixed effect)
beta1    <- mean(group_slopes)       # mean slope      (fixed effect)
sigma_u0 <- sd(group_intercepts)     # SD of random intercepts
sigma_u1 <- sd(group_slopes)         # SD of random slopes

x_seq  <- seq(min(df$x) - 5, max(df$x) + 5, length.out = 300)
mean_y <- beta0 + beta1 * x_seq

# 95% prediction interval for a NEW observation from a NEW group:
# Var(y_ij) = sigma_u0^2           <- random intercept variance
#           + sigma_u1^2 * x^2     <- random slope variance (fans with x)
#           + sigma_eps^2           <- residual variance
# 95% PI = mean ± 1.96 * sqrt(total variance)
total_sd <- sqrt(sigma_u0^2 + (sigma_u1 * x_seq)^2 + sigma_eps^2)

ribbon_df <- data.frame(
  x    = x_seq,
  y    = mean_y,
  ymin = mean_y - 1.96 * total_sd,
  ymax = mean_y + 1.96 * total_sd
)

# ── Formula ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
formula_label <- paste0(
  "<span style='font-size:15pt'>",
  "<b>Level 1 (Within-group):</b><br>",
  "<i>y<sub>ij</sub></i> = <i>β<sub>0j</sub></i> + <i>β<sub>1j</sub></i><i>x<sub>ij</sub></i> + <i>e<sub>ij</sub></i><br><br>",
  "<b>Level 2 (Between-group):</b><br>",
  "<i>β<sub>0j</sub></i> = <i>γ<sub>00</sub></i> + <i>u<sub>0j</sub></i><br>",
  "<i>β<sub>1j</sub></i> = <i>γ<sub>10</sub></i> + <i>u<sub>1j</sub></i>",
  "</span>"
)

# annotation x position for the bracket
ann_x <- quantile(df$x, 0.93)
idx   <- which.min(abs(x_seq - ann_x))

group_colors <- c("#E41A1C","#377EB8","#4DAF4A","#FF7F00","#984EA3")

p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y, color = group, group = group)) +
  
  # 95% prediction interval ribbon
  geom_ribbon(
    data = ribbon_df,
    aes(x = x, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill = "#00A9E0",
    alpha = 0.04
  ) +
  
  # Dashed black lines for PI borders
  geom_line(
    data = ribbon_df,
    aes(x = x, y = ymin),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = ribbon_df,
    aes(x = x, y = ymax),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # Raw scatter, faded
  geom_point(
    size = 3,
    alpha = 0.20
  ) +
  
  # Formula annotation
  annotate(
    "richtext",
    x = min(x_seq) + 1,
    y = max(ribbon_df$ymax),
    label = formula_label,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 0.60,
    fill = NA,
    label.color = NA,
    size = 4.5
  ) +
  
  # Bracket annotation for the band
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = ann_x,
    xend = ann_x,
    y = ribbon_df$ymin[idx],
    yend = ribbon_df$ymax[idx],
    color = "#EB7B35",
    linewidth = 1,
    arrow = arrow(
      ends = "both",
      length = unit(0.09, "inches"),
      type = "closed"
    )
  ) +
  
  # Mean line (fixed effects)
  geom_line(
    data = ribbon_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "#4B4F54",
    linewidth = 2
  ) +
  
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = ann_x + 1.5,
    y = mean_y[idx],
    label = "",
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 0.5,
    size = 3.5,
    color = "#00B5AD",
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(floor(min(df$x)), ceiling(max(df$x) + 5), by = 10),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.10, 0.25))
  ) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.title      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text       = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks      = element_line(color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  
  labs(x = "Predictor (X)", y = "Outcome (Y)")

#p

ggsave(here::here('slides','img','mlm_plot.png'), plot = p, width = 6.5, height = 4, dpi = 300)

# Seattle Children's official colors include:
#Teal ("#00B5AD")
#Bright Green ("#8DC63F")
#Light Blue ("#00A9E0")
#Warm Gray ("#A7A9AC")
#Dark Gray ("#4B4F54")
#orange ("#EB7B35")

```

.center[
<img src=`r here::here('slides','img','mlm_plot.png')` style="width:600px; display:block; margin:-40px auto -20px auto;">
]  

<small>

.center[
**Multilevel models do something different:**  
they *simultaneously* model variation **within** and **between** clusters.
]

</small>

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">  The Solution: Multilevel Models

<small>

**Model the structure - don't ignore it, don't destroy it.**

*All the approaches so far fall short in some way:*

- **Disaggregation:** <small>Easy but biased; treats all observations as exchangeable</small>  

- **Aggregation:** <small>Only higher level data; different questions</small>

- **Dummy codes:** <small>Assumes *one* shared story across clusters, unwieldy</small>

- **Post-adjustment:** <small>Corrects bias but ignores cluster variation</small>   

- **Separate regressions:** <small>Parameter-hungry and can't generalize</small>  

</small>

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">  The Solution: Multilevel Models

<small>

**Model dependency across observations** 

<small>

  - Longitudinal, clustered, or cross-classified data? `r emo::ji("white_check_mark")` No problem!  

  - Tailor your model to the **sources of correlation** in your data

</small>

**Include categorical or continuous predictors at any level**  

<small>

  - Time-varying, person-level, or group-level predictors   

  - Explore **why** observations are correlated, don’t just control for it

</small>

**Handle unbalanced or missing data**  

<small>

  - Not every person needs the same number of measurements  

  - Missing data? `r emo::ji("white_check_mark")` No problem!

</small>

</small>

---

class: center, middle, inverse

## Questions? `r emo::ji("thinking")`

.font120[
`r emo::ji("e-mail")` waylon.howard@seattlechildrens.org      
]

Next: *Diving into Multilevel Models (MLM)*
